reasoning_chain_text: 1. Aggregate Charter flight passengers by year for the Los Angeles international-airport passenger data from 2017-2022 and identify the year with the highest total.

2. Identify the NY counties that satisfy all four thresholds: at least 65 bridges rated Poor, at least 50 fire departments, at least 100 bank ATMs, and at least 1.0 MW of operational renewable energy capacity.
3. For the counties that remain after those four filters, determine each county seat.
4. Using violent-crime counts for the year from step 1, determine which remaining county had the higher count.
5. Determine the land company that developed the seat of the county identified in step 4.
6. Return the year that land company was founded.
original_final_question: Among NY counties that have at least 65 'Poor' bridges, at least 50 fire departments, at least 100 bank ATMs, and at least 1.0 MW of operational renewable energy capacity, pick the county with more violent crimes in the year within 2017-2022 when Charter flight passengers over Los Angeles international airports were highest. In what year was the land company founded that developed this county's seat? Please return the year only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=1, data.gov year derivation - node 1):
Node 1: LA Charter passengers -> Highest 2017-2022 is 2021
HOP 2 (d=4, data.gov intersection - nodes 2-5 all independent):
Node 2: Bridge conditions - NY counties with >=65 Poor bridges -> {Ulster (84), Erie (82), Westchester (68), Orange (67)}
Node 3: Fire departments - NY counties with >=50 fire depts -> {Suffolk (108), Erie (91), Nassau (70), Westchester (57), Onondaga (53), Orange (52), Ulster (51), Oneida (50)}

Node 4: Bank ATMs - NY counties with >=100 ATMs -> {NY (676), Nassau (515), Suffolk (494), Queens (429), Kings (388), Westchester (335), Erie (244), Monroe (232), Onondaga (182), Bronx (158), Albany (148), Orange (128), Dutchess (125), Richmond (115)}
Node 5: Renewable energy - NY counties with >=1.0 MW operational -> {Steuben (286.29), Chautauqua (185.90), Lewis (183.99), ..., Erie (11.48), ..., Westchester (2.64), ...}
Intersection(2,3,4,5) -> {Erie, Westchester}
Note: Ulster excluded by Node 4 (76 ATMs < 100) and Node 5 (0 MW renewable); Orange excluded by Node 5 (0 MW renewable)
HOP 3 (d=2, Wikipedia - nodes 6-7 BOTH depend on Hop 2 intersection):
Node 6: Erie County Wikipedia -> county seat is Buffalo
Node 7: Westchester County Wikipedia -> county seat is White Plains
Result: {Buffalo, White Plains} - the county seats of the two qualifying counties
HOP 4 (d=1, data.gov comparative - node 8 compares both counties from SAME dataset):
Node 8: Index crimes by county <node_1 answer> -> Erie (3,493) vs Westchester (1,520)
Comparative: 3,493 > 1,520 -> Erie has more violent crimes -> Erie County selected
HOP 5 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 9 depends on Hop 4 comparative result):
Node 9: Buffalo Wikipedia -> Holland Land Company developed the area around 1800
HOP 6 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 10 depends on node 9):
Node 10: Holland Land Company Wikipedia -> founded in 1789
Final answer: 1789
